mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) signals maximum monetary accommodation. Under this regime, equity valuations expand as discount rates compress and real yields turn negative, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09% as of 2020-04-15 indicates emergency monetary policy
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary policy regime
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 40% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 12-month rolling window during zero-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as forward guidance reprices downward.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 139,680k as of 2020-04-15 reflects pandemic-driven employment shock
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances following PAYEMS declines > 2%, or the correlation between PAYEMS changes and 5-day SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth accelerates above trend as consumer confidence recovers post-shock, signaling demand rebound that supports corporate earnings revisions upward, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 444,491M as of 2020-04-15 reflects early reopening demand signals
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS growth and 60-day SPX returns falls below 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window, or SPX produces negative returns in more than 50% of 60-day windows following RSAFS acceleration.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) contraction signals supply-side weakness and capacity utilization collapse. When combined with elevated unemployment (UNRATE > 5%), this indicates stagflationary pressure that compresses equity risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 105.07 and UNRATE at 5.8% as of 2020-04-15 reflect pandemic supply shock
regime_hint: supply-side shock with labor market slack
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when both INDPRO declines > 1.5% and UNRATE > 5%, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.15) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) surge above 1,000k units signals construction-led demand recovery and forward-looking confidence in residential investment. This demand impulse propagates through supply chains and employment, supporting equity valuations over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,017k as of 2020-04-15 indicates housing market stabilization
regime_hint: post-shock demand recovery with housing sector rebound
failure_condition: Rank IC between HOUST levels and 60-day SPX returns is below 0.03 over any 24-month rolling window, or SPX produces negative returns in more than 45% of 60-day windows when HOUST > 1,000k.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation expectations remain anchored (CPIAUCSL growth < 2.5% year-over-year) while monetary policy is maximally accommodative (FEDFUNDS near zero). This combination allows real rates to remain deeply negative, supporting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 237.63 and FEDFUNDS at 0.09% indicate low inflation with emergency easing
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 45% of instances when both conditions are met, or the interaction coefficient between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL is not statistically significant (p > 0.20).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises sharply above 5% in a single month, signaling acute labor market shock. This triggers immediate repricing of corporate earnings and equity risk premiums downward, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.8% as of 2020-04-15 reflects pandemic unemployment surge
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances following UNRATE spikes > 0.5pp, or the correlation between UNRATE changes and 5-day SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.12).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) contracts sharply (quarter-over-quarter annualized < -5%) while monetary policy remains at zero lower bound (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%). Under this regime, equity markets price in extended recovery period with sustained low rates, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as forward guidance stabilizes expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 16,150.6B and FEDFUNDS at 0.09% indicate severe contraction with emergency policy support
regime_hint: deep recession with maximum monetary accommodation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when both conditions are met, or the interaction term between GDPC1 and FEDFUNDS is not statistically significant (p > 0.18) in predicting 60-day returns.
